title: Heleophylax triquetrus
taxonomy: Heleophylax triquetrus is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The plant is classified as an herb, and its classification is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae. Within the kingdom Plantae, it falls under the phylum Angiospermophyta, and then under the class Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, it falls under the order Asterales, and then under the family Stylidiaceae.

intro: Heleophylax triquetrus, commonly known as the Tasmanian heath, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and leathery leaves. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of the Tasmanian heath include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Heleophylax triquetrus include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of skin conditions.
